MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Sworn Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPERANCES:
Steven B. Piser, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.

PLAINTIFF KAREN DELACRUZ' Motion To Compel Sworn Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco is heard this day. No opposition filed the Court adopts its Tentative Ruling.

Motion to compel further responses is granted as follows.
All objections are overruled. Defendant to serve verified responses to Requests #3,4,5,6, and 8 without objections by April 15, 2016. Response to be in compliance with CCP Section 2033.220. Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro-tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Not Reported. 302/JPT

Plaintiff Karen Delacruzs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Sworn Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPERANCES:
Steven B. Piser, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.

PLAINTIFF KAREN DELACRUZ' Motion And Motion To Compel Sworn Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is heard this day. There being no oppotion the Court adopts its Tentative Ruling.

Granted. All objections are overruled. The code requires that a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 The case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond to the interrogatories. The court finds that defendant did not meet their obligation.
Defendant to provide a response to each subpart of 15.1 and 20.6. Defendant to serve a verified response without objection no later then April 15, 2016.
Requests for sanctions is granted in the amount of $575 to be made payable to plaintiff and her attorney of record. Payment to be served on plaintiff counsel's office no later than April 15, 2016.
Order signed in open court.
Judge Pro-tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Not Reported. 302/JPT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Set For Trial On Oct-03-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
